hypothesis.thesis_statement: In A-shares, higher Book leverage (annual) predicts lower cross-sectional forward returns (US predictor 'BookLeverage', Fama and French 1992, JF). Test whether this published US anomaly ports to A-shares.
hypothesis.mechanism: OSAP economic category: leverage. Proposed A-share build: balance -> 'osap_bookleverage_ashare'. Definition (US): Total assets (at) divided by book value of equity plus deferred taxes (txditc) and preferred stock. Equity is shareholder equity (seq) if available, or book equity (ceq) plus preferred stock (pstk, if missing pstkrv, if missing pstkl), or total assets minus total liabilities (lt)

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: [DRAFT] The US 'BookLeverage' effect may not survive A-share microstructure (T+1, limit boards, retail-dominated flow) or may be subsumed by existing lev factors after neutralization.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: [DRAFT] That the US accounting/price construction maps faithfully to Tushare A-share fields without definitional drift.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: [DRAFT] Sealed-OOS rank-ICIR below the committed band, or hard rules failing after realistic costs.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: [DRAFT] Review turnover at the chosen rebalance before committing.

todo_before_registering: Implement factor 'osap_bookleverage_ashare' in factor_library (map US definition -> A-share Tushare/Qlib fields; PIT-safe Ref(...,1)).
CONFIRM not a duplicate of existing catalog factors in category 'lev' (heuristic flag = REVIEW).
SET expected_effect = YOUR A-share prediction (point + CI + horizon). Do NOT copy us_evidence_context_only above.
CONFIRM the OOS window is unburned for this design before registering.
